title: May a Muslim woman teach boys and girls in a co-ed school?
stanceSummary: The Shari'ah sets core rules: no inappropriate privacy (khalwa), modest dress and speech, and dignified interaction. Beyond that, gender mixing in institutions is partly **culture-dependent**: what is normal in one society may be alien in another. A female teacher in a supervised classroom, adhering to Islamic guidelines, is not automatically forbidden solely because male students are present—especially when local professional norms require it.
transcript:
## Summary of Yasir Qadhi's Position

In a [2020 video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UL4lvUdCOmA) (starting at [0:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UL4lvUdCOmA&t=0)), Yasir Qadhi addresses this question.

- Fixed rules: **no khalwa**, **hijab/adab**, **no flirtation**.
- Beyond that, **degree of mixing** follows **cultural** and **professional** context.
- **Classroom teaching** with many present differs from **private** mixed settings.

---
